data.title: KLA Corp. (NASDAQ: KLAC) shares drop after releasing Q2 earnings. Earnings per share: $8.85. Quarterly revenue: $3.3 billion.
data.description: Shares of KLA Corp. (NASDAQ: KLAC) dipped in after-hours trading on Thursday following the release of its second-quarter earnings report. The company reported earnings of $8.85 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of $8.80, as reported by Benzinga Pro. Additionally, KLA recorded quarterly revenue of $3.3 billion, exceeding the analyst consensus. For more information, refer to Benzinga.com for the complete story. Keep an eye on KLAC stock for further price movements.

data.title: Investor rights law firm urges Block, Inc. shareholders to discuss their rights regarding possible breaches by officers. Contact immediately. (Numbers: Jan. 29, 2026, NY, NYSE:XYZ)
data.description: Investors are advised to promptly reach out to the company as time may be running out to assert their rights. On January 29, 2026, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm specializing in investor rights, announced an inquiry into potential breaches by specific officers and directors at Block, Inc. (NYSE: XYZ). It is crucial for shareholders to act swiftly to determine and safeguard their interests.

data.title: i-80 Gold adds Ronald Butler Jr., Michael Jalonen, and Steven Yopps to board as independent directors. Effective Feb 1, 2026.
data.description: In a recent development, i-80 Gold Corp. revealed the addition of Ronald Butler Jr., Michael Jalonen, and Steven Yopps to its Board as independent directors, with their term commencing on February 1, 2026. This move comes as i-80 Gold Corp. aims to strengthen its governance structure. The company, listed on both the Toronto Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol IAU and the NYSE American as IAUX, is welcoming the new appointments as part of its ongoing efforts to enhance its leadership team. The trio of directors are set to bring a wealth of experience and expertise to the boardroom, further solidifying i-80 Gold Corp.'s position in the market.

data.title: Fatina Brave appointed VP of infrastructure and sustainability at Ingalls Shipbuilding, replacing George Nungesser after 37 years.
data.description: In PASCAGOULA, Mississippi, on January 29, 2026, HII disclosed that Fatina Brave has been named the new vice president of infrastructure and sustainability at its Ingalls Shipbuilding division. Brave takes over from Eric Crooker, who has shifted to the position of vice president of program management at Ingalls. Crooker is replacing George Nungesser, who will retire in early February after a tenure of 37 years with the company.

data.title: Blue Ridge Bankshares, Inc. reports profitable 2025 results, NYSE: BRBS, with Consent Order termination.
data.description: In a milestone achievement, Richmond-based Blue Ridge Bankshares, Inc. (NYSE American: BRBS) announced its return to profitability after a year of significant losses. The holding company of Blue Ridge Bank, National Association, successfully terminated the previously imposed consent order. This marks a crucial turning point for the Company, with shareholders and investors eagerly awaiting the official financial report.The Company's journey to recovery and success can be seen as a testament to their resilience and strategic decisions in a challenging market environment.

data.title: Koss Corporation, a high-fidelity headphone company, reports second quarter results for the period ended December 31, 2025.
data.description: Koss Corporation, a U.S. high-fidelity headphone company, recently disclosed its financial performance for the quarter concluding on December 31, 2025. The corporation, headquartered in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, operates under the NASDAQ symbol KOSS.

data.title: Birks Group reports 11.8% increase in net sales for FY2026 Holiday Period, with 2.5% rise in comparable store sales.
data.description: Montreal-based Birks Group Inc. (NYSE American: BGI) announced an 11.8% rise in net sales for the 8-week FY2026 Holiday Period ending December 27, 2025. The company also reported a 2.5% growth in comparable store sales during the same period compared to FY2025. The increase in net sales during the holiday period is a positive sign for Birks Group's financial performance.

data.title: "Influential Women exceeds 50K LinkedIn followers, a milestone in platform growth spotlighting women leaders. -PRNewswire"
data.description: In St. Petersburg, Florida, on January 29, 2026, Influential Women, a rapidly expanding media platform that features women leaders, founders, and experts from various fields, has recently reached over 50,000 followers on LinkedIn. This achievement signifies a significant milestone in the platform's ongoing growth and impact.

data.title: The Olympic Truce begins on January 30, with the Truce Foundation introducing an Online Truce Compliance Index.
data.description: The "Geolympics" explores the impact of the Olympic Truce on geopolitics as The Truce Foundation introduces the Online Truce Compliance Index. This index monitors nations' adherence to the Olympic Truce, upholding a tradition that dates back 2,800 years. In a recent development in Milan on Jan. 29, 2026, the foundation aims to bring accountability to this ancient practice.

data.title: Kansas House passes HB 2476, supporting farmers' access to essential crop protection tools - critical for agriculture.
data.description: The passage of HB 2476 by the House of Representatives in Kansas demonstrates their support for farmers by protecting vital agricultural tools. This significant bill secures farmers' access to crucial crop protection methods. The vote took place on Jan. 29, 2026, in Omaha, Nebraska. This legislation aims to safeguard farmers' ability to utilize essential tools for crop management.

data.title: Rithm Capital Corp declared taxable dividends of $1 per share for its common stock in 2025.
data.description: In New York, Rithm Capital Corp. (NYSE:RITM) disclosed the tax implications for its common and preferred stock dividends subject to taxation in 2025. Reportedly, taxable dividends for Rithm Capital's common stock (CUSIP #64828T201) for the year amount to around $1.00000 per share. The tax breakdown for common stock distributions during the year is outlined below: Record Date / Pay Date (1), Form, Ordinary Dividend, and Qualified Dividend.

data.title: MFA Financial, Inc. (NYSE: MFA) posts dividend tax info for 2025 on website www.mfafinancial.com. Stockholders report distributions on 2025 tax returns.
data.description: In New York, MFA Financial, Inc. (NYSE: MFA) has revealed details about its dividend distributions for the tax year ending December 31, 2025. Information on all common stock dividend payments made throughout 2025 can be found on the company's website at www.mfafinancial.com. Shareholders are advised that these distributions must be reported on their 2025 federal income tax returns. As per the federal income tax regulations for real estate investment trusts (“REITs”), it is anticipated that MFA’s common stock dividend distributions for 2025 will be subject to these rules.
